subject: [PATCH] sched/psi: use for_each_set_bit() in psi_group_change() task-count walk

reviews.author: K Nayak
reviews.summary:
Reviewer K Nayak noted that the use of for_each_set_bit() in psi_group_change() task-count walk does not handle the case where NR_PSI_TASK_COUNTS is greater than BITS_PER_LONG, which could lead to incorrect results.

Reviewer suggested converting the arguments to unsigned long instead of assigning them to local variables, and agreed that for_each_set_bit() is cleaner.
reviews.raw_body:
Hello Usama,

On 7/14/2026 7:50 PM, Usama Arif wrote:

---

nit.

Can't we convert the arguments to unsigned long instead of assigning
them to these local variables?

Apart form that, for_each_set_bit() is indeed much cleaner. Feel free
to include:

Reviewed-by: K Prateek Nayak <kprateek.nayak@amd.com>

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: Author addressed a concern about using for_each_set_bit() in psi_group_change(), preferring explicit conversion and citing caller consistency, but did not commit to revising the patch.
sentiment_signals: author's response does not indicate a fix is planned
reviews.raw_body:
I kind of prefer the explicit conversion. Also all the callers take int
instead of long. The printk would also change from %x to %lx.

The assignment itself should hopefully be free? The compiler hopefully
optimizes it away.

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner
reviews.summary: Reviewer Johannes Weiner noted that the original implementation used ffs (fast finding of first set bit) which was hand-tuned to scheduler benchmarks, and expressed concern that the new for_each_set_bit() approach may produce more code due to GCC's optimization, suggesting careful review of the generated assembly and benchmarking.
reviews.raw_body:
This actually started out using ffs. Because the performance is so
sensitive in this path, this was handtuned to scheduler benchmarks.

https://lore.kernel.org/all/20180718120318.GC2476@hirez.programming.kicks-ass.net/

Not the worst idea to revisit this, but you have to be careful, look
at the asm, and benchmark it. gcc is producing more code for me with
your patch.

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary:
Author Usama Arif addressed Johannes Weiner's concern about the efficiency of using for_each_set_bit() in psi_group_change(), citing benchmark results that show __ffs performs best, and offered to send a revised patch (v2) if it looks okay.

The author is addressing a concern about the efficiency of the task-count walk in psi_group_change(). They are proposing to use __ffs() + m &= m-1 form, which iterates only over the set bits and terminates naturally on m == 0. The author has provided microbench results showing that this approach reduces the cost by 47-63% compared to the original code.

2026-07-15.patch_summary: This patch improves the efficiency and readability of the psi_group_change() function in the Linux kernel's scheduler by replacing two loops that iterate over bitmasks with more efficient and easier-to-read calls to for_each_set_bit(). The original code would visit every position up to the highest set bit, even if it was sparse, resulting in unnecessary iterations. The new implementation uses a single loop to iterate only over the set bits, reducing the number of iterations and improving performance on hot scheduler workloads.
